subject: Economics
chapter: Simple Applications of Tools of Demand and Supply

questions.q: What is the law of demand? Explain it with the help of a schedule.
questions.answer: The law of demand states that other things remaining constant (ceteris paribus), if the price of a commodity falls, the quantity demanded of it will rise and vice versa. This can be explained with the help of a demand schedule which shows the different quantities of a commodity that consumers are willing to buy at different prices.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: When the price of a commodity rises, the quantity supplied of it _______ (increases/decreases).
Explain the concept of supply. What are the factors that affect it?
questions.answer: Supply refers to the quantity of a commodity that producers are willing and able to produce and sell at a given price level. The factors that affect supply include price of the commodity, cost of production, technology, government policies, and expectations of future prices.

questions.q: State whether the following statement is true or false: 'The supply curve always slopes upwards from left to right'.
What is the difference between a movement along the supply curve and a shift of the supply curve?
questions.answer: A movement along the supply curve occurs when the price of the commodity changes, resulting in a change in the quantity supplied. A shift of the supply curve occurs when any factor other than the price of the commodity changes, resulting in a change in the supply of the commodity.

questions.q: Explain the concept of equilibrium price with the help of a diagram.
questions.answer: The equilibrium price is the price at which the quantity demanded of a commodity is equal to the quantity supplied. This can be shown graphically by the intersection of the demand and supply curves. At this point, the market is said to be in equilibrium.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The law of supply states that other things remaining constant (ceteris paribus), if the price of a commodity _______ (rises/falls), the quantity supplied of it will rise.
State the assumptions of the law of demand.
questions.answer: The assumptions of the law of demand include that the income of the consumer remains constant, the prices of other commodities remain constant, the taste and preferences of the consumer remain constant, and there is no change in the consumer's expectations.

questions.q: What is the effect of an increase in the price of a substitute good on the demand for a commodity? Explain with the help of a diagram.
questions.answer: An increase in the price of a substitute good will lead to an increase in the demand for the commodity. This can be shown graphically by a rightward shift of the demand curve.

questions.q: Which of the following is a determinant of demand? (a) Price of the commodity, (b) Income of the consumer, (c) Price of a complementary good, (d) All of the above
